req.title: Data retention
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for resource retention policy and nightly purge job requirements.

Requirement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be
purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application models)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class RetentionPolicy(Enum):
    SHORT_TERM = "short_term"       # 30 days
    MEDIUM_TERM = "medium_term"     # 90 days
    LONG_TERM = "long_term"         # 365 days
    PERMANENT = "permanent"         # never expires


POLICY_DURATION_DAYS: dict[RetentionPolicy, Optional[int]] = {
    RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM: 30,
    RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM: 90,
    RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM: 365,
    RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT: None,
}


@dataclass
class Resource:
    id: str
    name: str
    policy: RetentionPolicy
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    purged_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    is_purged: bool = False

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if self.expires_at is None and self.policy != RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            days = POLICY_DURATION_DAYS[self.policy]
            self.expires_at = self.created_at + timedelta(days=days)

    def is_expired(self, as_of: Optional[datetime] = None) -> bool:
        if self.policy == RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            return False
        reference = as_of or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return self.expires_at is not None and reference >= self.expires_at


@dataclass
class AuditEntry:
    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    action: str                 # e.g. "PURGE", "RETAIN", "SKIP"
    resource_id: str
    resource_name: str
    policy: str
    reason: str
    performed_by: str           # e.g. "nightly_purge_job"
    success: bool
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Interfaces / service contracts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ResourceRepository:
    def list_expired(self, as_of: datetime) -> list[Resource]: ...
    def list_active(self) -> list[Resource]: ...
    def delete(self, resource_id: str) -> None: ...
    def get(self, resource_id: str) -> Optional[Resource]: ...


class AuditRepository:
    def save(self, entry: AuditEntry) -> None: ...
    def find_by_resource(self, resource_id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]: ...
    def find_by_job_run(self, run_id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]: ...


class PurgeJobRunner:
    """Orchestrates the nightly purge."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        resource_repo: ResourceRepository,
        audit_repo: AuditRepository,
        clock=None,
    ):
        self.resource_repo = resource_repo
        self.audit_repo = audit_repo
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def run(self, run_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
        run_id = run_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        now = self._clock()
        expired = self.resource_repo.list_expired(as_of=now)

        results = {"run_id": run_id, "purged": [], "failed": [], "skipped": []}

        for resource in expired:
            entry_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            try:
                self.resource_repo.delete(resource.id)
                audit = AuditEntry(
                    id=entry_id,
                    timestamp=now,
                    action="PURGE",
                    resource_id=resource.id,
                    resource_name=resource.name,
                    policy=resource.policy.value,
                    reason=f"Resource expired at {resource.expires_at}",
                    performed_by="nightly_purge_job",
                    success=True,
                )
                self.audit_repo.save(audit)
                results["purged"].append(resource.id)

            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
                audit = AuditEntry(
                    id=entry_id,
                    timestamp=now,
                    action="PURGE",
                    resource_id=resource.id,
                    resource_name=resource.name,
                    policy=resource.policy.value,
                    reason=f"Resource expired at {resource.expires_at}",
                    performed_by="nightly_purge_job",
                    success=False,
                    error_message=str(exc),
                )
                self.audit_repo.save(audit)
                results["failed"].append(resource.id)

        return results


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOW = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 2, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)  # 02:00 UTC – nightly window


def _make_resource(
    policy: RetentionPolicy = RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM,
    days_old: int = 0,
    resource_id: Optional[str] = None,
    name: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Resource:
    created = NOW - timedelta(days=days_old)
    return Resource(
        id=resource_id or str(uuid.uuid4()),
        name=name or f"resource-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        policy=policy,
        created_at=created,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def resource_repo() -> MagicMock:
    return MagicMock(spec=ResourceRepository)


@pytest.fixture()
def audit_repo() -> MagicMock:
    return MagicMock(spec=AuditRepository)


@pytest.fixture()
def job(resource_repo, audit_repo) -> PurgeJobRunner:
    return PurgeJobRunner(
        resource_repo=resource_repo,
        audit_repo=audit_repo,
        clock=lambda: NOW,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 1 – Retention policy assignment
# ===========================================================================

class TestRetentionPolicyAssignment:
    """Resources must be assigned an appropriate retention policy."""

    def test_short_term_policy_expires_in_30_days(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=0)
        expected_expiry = resource.created_at + timedelta(days=30)
        assert resource.expires_at == expected_expiry

    def test_medium_term_policy_expires_in_90_days(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM, days_old=0)
        expected_expiry = resource.created_at + timedelta(days=90)
        assert resource.expires_at == expected_expiry

    def test_long_term_policy_expires_in_365_days(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM, days_old=0)
        expected_expiry = resource.created_at + timedelta(days=365)
        assert resource.expires_at == expected_expiry

    def test_permanent_policy_has_no_expiry(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT, days_old=0)
        assert resource.expires_at is None

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("policy", list(RetentionPolicy))
    def test_all_policies_produce_valid_resource(self, policy):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=policy)
        assert resource.id
        assert resource.policy == policy
        if policy == RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            assert resource.expires_at is None
        else:
            assert resource.expires_at is not None
            assert resource.expires_at > resource.created_at


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 2 – Expiry detection
# ===========================================================================

class TestExpiryDetection:
    """is_expired must correctly reflect the current time vs expires_at."""

    def test_resource_not_expired_before_expiry_date(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=10)
        assert not resource.is_expired(as_of=NOW)

    def test_resource_expired_exactly_at_expiry_boundary(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=30)
        assert resource.is_expired(as_of=resource.expires_at)

    def test_resource_expired_after_expiry_date(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        assert resource.is_expired(as_of=NOW)

    def test_permanent_resource_never_expires(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT, days_old=9999)
        assert not resource.is_expired(as_of=NOW)

    def test_resource_not_expired_one_second_before_boundary(self):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=30)
        one_second_before = resource.expires_at - timedelta(seconds=1)
        assert not resource.is_expired(as_of=one_second_before)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("days_over", [1, 7, 30, 365, 3650])
    def test_resource_expired_various_overdue_intervals(self, days_over):
        resource = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=30 + days_over)
        assert resource.is_expired(as_of=NOW)


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 3 – Nightly purge job: scheduling & scope
# ===========================================================================

class TestNightlyPurgeScope:
    """Only expired resources are purged; active and permanent ones are left alone."""

    def test_expired_resources_are_purged(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        result = job.run()

        resource_repo.delete.assert_called_once_with(expired.id)
        assert expired.id in result["purged"]

    def test_active_resources_are_not_deleted(self, job, resource_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []

        job.run()

        resource_repo.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_permanent_resources_are_not_included_in_expired_list(self):
        permanent = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT, days_old=1000)
        assert not permanent.is_expired(as_of=NOW)

    def test_multiple_expired_resources_all_purged(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired_resources = [
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31),
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM, days_old=91),
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM, days_old=366),
        ]
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = expired_resources

        result = job.run()

        assert resource_repo.delete.call_count == 3
        assert len(result["purged"]) == 3

    def test_mixed_expired_and_active_only_expired_purged(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        result = job.run()

        assert expired.id in result["purged"]
        assert len(result["purged"]) == 1

    def test_empty_repository_produces_no_purges(self, job, resource_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []

        result = job.run()

        resource_repo.delete.assert_not_called()
        assert result["purged"] == []
        assert result["failed"] == []

    def test_list_expired_is_called_with_current_timestamp(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []

        job.run()

        resource_repo.list_expired.assert_called_once_with(as_of=NOW)


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 4 – Audit trail: completeness
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditTrailCompleteness:
    """Every purge action (success or failure) must produce an audit entry."""

    def test_successful_purge_creates_audit_entry(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        audit_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_entry_action_is_purge(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.action == "PURGE"

    def test_audit_entry_contains_correct_resource_id(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.resource_id == expired.id

    def test_audit_entry_contains_resource_name(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(
            policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31, name="my-important-file"
        )
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.resource_name == "my-important-file"

    def test_audit_entry_contains_policy_name(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM, days_old=91)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.policy == RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM.value

    def test_audit_entry_timestamp_matches_job_run_time(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.timestamp == NOW

    def test_audit_entry_performed_by_identifies_nightly_job(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.performed_by == "nightly_purge_job"

    def test_audit_entry_reason_mentions_expiry_date(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert str(expired.expires_at) in entry.reason

    def test_audit_entry_has_unique_id(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resources = [
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31),
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=35),
        ]
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = resources

        job.run()

        ids = [call_args[0][0].id for call_args in audit_repo.save.call_args_list]
        assert len(set(ids)) == 2, "Each audit entry must have a unique ID"

    def test_one_audit_entry_per_purged_resource(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resources = [_make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31) for _ in range(5)]
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = resources

        job.run()

        assert audit_repo.save.call_count == 5

    def test_no_audit_entries_when_nothing_to_purge(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []

        job.run()

        audit_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_entry_success_flag_true_on_success(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.success is True

    def test_audit_entry_no_error_message_on_success(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.error_message is None


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 5 – Audit trail: failure handling
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditTrailOnFailure:
    """Even when a purge fails, the audit trail must record the failure."""

    def test_failed_purge_still_writes_audit_entry(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]
        resource_repo.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("Storage backend unavailable")

        job.run()

        audit_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_failed_purge_audit_entry_has_success_false(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]
        resource_repo.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("Disk full")

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert entry.success is False

    def test_failed_purge_audit_entry_contains_error_message(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]
        resource_repo.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("Permission denied")

        job.run()

        entry: AuditEntry = audit_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert "Permission denied" in entry.error_message

    def test_failed_purge_reported_in_results_failed_list(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        expired = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [expired]
        resource_repo.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("Network error")

        result = job.run()

        assert expired.id in result["failed"]
        assert expired.id not in result["purged"]

    def test_failure_in_one_resource_does_not_abort_remaining(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        failing = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31, name="bad")
        succeeding = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=32, name="good")
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [failing, succeeding]
        resource_repo.delete.side_effect = [RuntimeError("Transient error"), None]

        result = job.run()

        assert failing.id in result["failed"]
        assert succeeding.id in result["purged"]
        assert audit_repo.save.call_count == 2


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 6 – Job run identity & traceability
# ===========================================================================

class TestJobRunIdentity:
    """Each job run must be uniquely identifiable for end-to-end traceability."""

    def test_job_returns_run_id(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []
        result = job.run()
        assert "run_id" in result
        assert result["run_id"]

    def test_caller_supplied_run_id_is_preserved(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []
        custom_id = "scheduled-run-20240615T020000Z"
        result = job.run(run_id=custom_id)
        assert result["run_id"] == custom_id

    def test_auto_generated_run_ids_are_unique_across_runs(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []
        run_ids = {job.run()["run_id"] for _ in range(10)}
        assert len(run_ids) == 10


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 7 – Idempotency & boundary conditions
# ===========================================================================

class TestIdempotencyAndBoundaries:
    """Edge-case and boundary tests for correctness."""

    def test_resource_expiring_exactly_now_is_purged(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource = Resource(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            name="boundary-resource",
            policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM,
            created_at=NOW - timedelta(days=30),
            expires_at=NOW,  # expires exactly NOW
        )
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [resource]

        result = job.run()

        assert resource.id in result["purged"]

    def test_resource_expiring_one_second_in_future_is_not_purged(self):
        resource = Resource(
            id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            name="almost-expired",
            policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM,
            created_at=NOW - timedelta(days=30),
            expires_at=NOW + timedelta(seconds=1),
        )
        assert not resource.is_expired(as_of=NOW)

    def test_resource_with_very_old_expiry_is_purged(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        ancient = _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=3650)
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = [ancient]

        result = job.run()

        assert ancient.id in result["purged"]

    def test_running_job_twice_queries_repo_both_times(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = []

        job.run()
        job.run()

        assert resource_repo.list_expired.call_count == 2

    def test_large_batch_all_audited(self, job, resource_repo, audit_repo):
        resources = [
            _make_resource(policy=RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, days_old=31)
            for _ in range(100)
        ]
        resource_repo.list_expired.return_value = resources

        result = job.run()

        assert len(result["purged"]) == 100
        assert audit_repo.save.call_count == 100


# ===========================================================================
# SECTION 8 – Policy duration contract
# ===========================================================================

class TestPolicyDurationContract:
    """Verify the retention duration table is authoritative and complete."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("policy,expected_days", [
        (RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM, 30),
        (RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM, 90),
        (RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM, 365),
        (RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT, None),
    ])
    def test_policy_duration_mapping(self, policy, expected_days):
        assert POLICY_DURATION_DAYS[policy] == expected_days

    def test_all_policies_represented_in_duration_table(self):
        for policy in RetentionPolicy:
            assert policy in POLICY_DURATION_DAYS, (
                f"Policy {policy} is missing from POLICY_DURATION_DAYS"
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("policy", [p for p in RetentionPolicy if p != RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT])
    def test_non_permanent_policies_have_positive_duration(self, policy):
        assert POLICY_DURATION_DAYS[policy] > 0
```

---

## What each section covers

| Section | Requirement aspect tested |
|---|---|
| **1 – Policy assignment** | Every policy maps to the correct `expires_at` at construction time |
| **2 – Expiry detection** | Boundary conditions (exact second, before, after), permanent resources |
| **3 – Purge scope** | Only expired resources are deleted; active / permanent ones are untouched |
| **4 – Audit trail (success)** | Presence, completeness, and uniqueness of every audit field on success |
| **5 – Audit trail (failure)** | Failures are recorded, not silently dropped; job continues processing |
| **6 – Run identity** | Each nightly run has a traceable ID; caller can supply their own |
| **7 – Idempotency & boundaries** | Exact-boundary expiry, very old resources, large batches |
| **8 – Policy contract** | The duration table is exhaustive and internally consistent |

doc._raw_response:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for resource retention policy and nightly purge job requirements.

Requirement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be
purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application models)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class RetentionPolicy(Enum):
    SHORT_TERM = "short_term"       # 30 days
    MEDIUM_TERM = "medium_term"     # 90 days
    LONG_TERM = "long_term"         # 365 days
    PERMANENT = "permanent"         # never expires


POLICY_DURATION_DAYS: dict[RetentionPolicy, Optional[int]] = {
    RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM: 30,
    RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM: 90,
    RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM: 365,
    RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT: None,
}


@dataclass
class Resource:
    id: str
    name: str
    policy: RetentionPolicy
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    purged_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    is_purged: bool = False

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if self.expires_at is None and self.policy != RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            days = POLICY_DURATION_DAYS[self.policy]
            self.expires_at = self.created_at + timedelta(days=days)

    def is_expired(self, as_of: Optional[datetime] = None) -> bool:
        if self.policy == RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            return False
        reference = as_of or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return self.expires_at is not None and reference >= self.expires_at


@dataclass
class AuditEntry:
    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    action: str                 # e.g. "PURGE", "RETAIN", "SKIP"
    resource_id: str
    resource_name

"""
Pytest tests for resource retention policy and nightly purge job requirements.

Requirement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be
purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application models)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class RetentionPolicy(Enum):
    SHORT_TERM = "short_term"       # 30 days
    MEDIUM_TERM = "medium_term"     # 90 days
    LONG_TERM = "long_term"         # 365 days
    PERMANENT = "permanent"         # never expires


POLICY_DURATION_DAYS: dict[RetentionPolicy, Optional[int]] = {
    RetentionPolicy.SHORT_TERM: 30,
    RetentionPolicy.MEDIUM_TERM: 90,
    RetentionPolicy.LONG_TERM: 365,
    RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT: None,
}


@dataclass
class Resource:
    id: str
    name: str
    policy: RetentionPolicy
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    purged_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    is_purged: bool = False

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if self.expires_at is None and self.policy != RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            days = POLICY_DURATION_DAYS[self.policy]
            self.expires_at = self.created_at + timedelta(days=days)

    def is_expired(self, as_of: Optional[datetime] = None) -> bool:
        if self.policy == RetentionPolicy.PERMANENT:
            return False
        reference = as_of or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return self.expires_at is not None and reference >= self.expires_at


@dataclass
class AuditEntry:
    id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    action: str                 # e.g. "PURGE", "RETAIN", "SKIP"
    resource_id: str
    resource_name: str
    policy: str
    reason: str
    performed_by: str           # e.g. "nightly_purge_job"
    success: bool
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Interfaces / service contracts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ResourceRepository:
    def list_expired(self, as_of: datetime) -> list[Resource]: ...
    def list_active(self) -> list[Resource]: ...
    def delete(self, resource_id: str) -> None: ...
    def get(self, resource_id: str) -> Optional[Resource]: ...


class AuditRepository:
    def save(self, entry: AuditEntry) -> None: ...
    def find_by_resource(self, resource_id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]: ...
    def find_by_job_run(self, run_id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]: ...


class PurgeJobRunner:
    """Orchestrates the nightly purge."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        resource_repo: ResourceRepository,
        audit_repo: AuditRepository,
        clock=None,
    ):
        self.resource_repo = resource_repo
        self.audit_repo = audit_repo
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def run(self, run_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
        run_id = run_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        now = self._clock()
        expired = self.resource_repo.list_expired(as_of=now)

        results = {"run_id": run_id, "purged": [], "failed": [], "skipped": []}

        for resource in expired:
            entry_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            try:
                self.resource_repo.delete(resource.id)
                audit = AuditEntry(
                    id=entry_id,
                    timestamp=now,
                    action="PURGE",
                    resource_id=resource.id,
                    resource_name=resource.name,
                    policy=resource.policy.value,
                    reason=f"Resource expired a
